Title: Peanuts that keep aflatoxin at bay: a threshold that matters

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Extracellular space/apoplast in the Ec construct
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed during pod development stages R5, R6 and R7 and maintained up to 72 hpi in infected cotyledons
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Food safety hazard and potent toxic/carcinogenic contaminant
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression restricted A. flavus invasion/colonization and reduced aflatoxin accumulation to near-undetectable levels in several lines
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MsDef1-overexpressing peanut lines showed very low fungal load relative to WT. 2. Several OE lines accumulated <1 to 4 ppb aflatoxin B1 compared with >1200 ppb in WT. 3. Defensin expression was associated with reduced fungal aflatoxin-pathway transcript abundance and altered fungal morphology.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in peanut; qRT-PCR expression analysis; fungal bioassay; qPCR fungal load estimation; HPLC aflatoxin quantification; microscopy
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: A. flavus infection of mature cotyledons, mainly 72 hpi; strains AF11-4, A-12 and A-191
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising antifungal transgene for reducing preharvest infection and aflatoxin contamination; potentially stack with HIGS against aflatoxin genes
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No morphological or developmental abnormalities reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts by enhancing host resistance to fungal invasion rather than directly altering host nutrient biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcript profiling by qRT-PCR; no public accession reported

Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Extracellular space/apoplast (Ec construct) or endoplasmic reticulum (ER construct)
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed during pod development stages R5, R6 and R7 and maintained up to 72 hpi in infected cotyledons
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Food safety hazard and potent toxic/carcinogenic contaminant
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression strongly suppressed A. flavus growth and reduced aflatoxin accumulation to near-immune levels in several events
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtDef4.2 OE lines had much lower fungal biomass than WT and HIGS lines. 2. Multiple Def4-Ec and Def4-ER events accumulated <2 ppb aflatoxin B1, with some <1 ppb. 3. Infection by A. flavus on OE lines showed reduced conidiophore length, altered conidial head width, and reduced aflatoxin-pathway gene expression.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in peanut; qRT-PCR expression analysis; fungal bioassay; qPCR fungal load estimation; HPLC aflatoxin quantification; microscopy
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: A. flavus infection of mature cotyledons, mainly 72 hpi; strains AF11-4, A-12 and A-191
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value antifungal transgene for peanut aflatoxin management; likely useful in stacked designs with HIGS constructs
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No morphological or developmental abnormalities reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Both apoplast-targeted and ER-retained forms were tested; OE lines showed a strong correlation between reduced fungal load and reduced aflatoxin content
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcript profiling by qRT-PCR; no public accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a middle-step conversion in aflatoxin biosynthesis associated with ver-1/aflM function, required for progression toward aflatoxin B1/B2 production
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Fungal cytoplasm; targeted to aflatoxisome/vesicle during aflatoxin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcription coincides with onset of aflatoxin biosynthesis during infection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Food safety hazard and potent toxic/carcinogenic contaminant
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for progression toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway enzyme targeted for silencing; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HIGS targeting aflM markedly reduced aflatoxin accumulation in infected peanut seeds to low ppb levels without restricting fungal colonization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hp-ver1(HIGS against aflM) lines accumulated 1–4 ppb or up to 20 ppb aflatoxin B1, far below WT. 2. aflM transcript levels in infecting A. flavus were reduced by 75.0%–97.0% in HIGS lines. 3. Silencing aflM was associated with reduced expression of other aflatoxin pathway genes and altered vesicle/aflatoxisome features.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Host-induced gene silencing in peanut; RT-PCR detection of hpRNA; qRT-PCR of fungal aflM and other cluster genes in infected cotyledons; HPLC aflatoxin profiling; microscopy of fungal vesicles
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: A. flavus infection of mature peanut cotyledons at 72 hpi; strains AF11-4, A-12 and A-191
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful HIGS target for transgenic peanut with very low aflatoxin contamination; likely best deployed together with antifungal genes to couple toxin suppression with reduced fungal invasion
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Did not reduce fungal colonization per se; no obvious agronomic abnormalities reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene source is fungal, whereas silencing construct is expressed in peanut; inhibition likely affected both upstream and downstream aflatoxin genes and aflatoxisome development
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcript profiling by qRT-PCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: AF11-4 isolate used for expression assays; also tested with A-12 and A-191

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Late methylated intermediate leading to aflatoxin B1/B2
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a late methylation step in the aflatoxin pathway associated with omtA/aflP function, required for final aflatoxin formation
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Fungal cytoplasm; targeted to aflatoxisome/vesicle during aflatoxin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcription coincides with onset of aflatoxin biosynthesis during infection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Food safety hazard and potent toxic/carcinogenic contaminant
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for final conversion toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key late-pathway enzyme targeted for silencing; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HIGS targeting aflP strongly reduced aflatoxin accumulation in infected peanut seeds, often to low ppb range, despite substantial fungal growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hp-omtA lines accumulated 1–20 ppb aflatoxin B1 versus very high levels in WT. 2. aflP transcript levels in infecting A. flavus were reduced by 82.0%–99.0% in HIGS lines. 3. Silencing aflP correlated with reduced aflD, aflM, aflR and aflX expression and weaker aflatoxisome staining.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Host-induced gene silencing in peanut; RT-PCR detection of hpRNA; qRT-PCR of fungal aflP and other cluster genes; HPLC aflatoxin quantification; microscopy
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: A. flavus infection of mature peanut cotyledons at 72 hpi; strains AF11-4, A-12 and A-191
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering aflatoxin-safe peanut; should be combined with antifungal resistance traits for more complete protection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Did not noticeably limit fungal invasion; no agronomic penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Fungal enzyme was silenced from the host via hpRNA; authors infer perturbation of aflatoxin packaging/export through aflatoxisome defects
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcript profiling by qRT-PCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: AF11-4 isolate used for expression assays; also tested with A-12 and A-191
